description: Analysis of a single LeetCode solution. Fields are ordered so the model reasons before it concludes.

solution_summary.description: 1-2 sentences describing what approach this code actually takes, read from the code itself (not assumed from the problem). Establishes shared understanding before any judgment.

input_caveats.description: If the problem statement is missing, ambiguous, or does not match the submitted code, state the mismatch here and analyze cautiously. Null if the input is clear and consistent.

reasoning.description: Why the code is or is not correct, grounded in its actual logic. Reason here before committing to an assessment.

edge_cases.description: Specific edge cases this code handles or fails: empty input, single element, duplicates, negatives, overflow, and problem-specific boundaries.

reasoning.description: Derive complexity from the actual loops, recursion, and data structures in THIS code. Identify what drives the bound BEFORE stating it. Note any hidden costs.

time.description: Big-O time, e.g. O(n log n).

space.description: Big-O space; note whether output is included.

strengths.description: Specific things done well in this code, not generic praise.

issue.description: The specific problem, ideally pointing at a line, name, or construct.

improvements.description: Concrete, specific to this code: style, redundancy, naming, robustness. No generic advice.

approach.description: Name and how it works, in 1-2 sentences.

reasoning.description: Why this complexity, derived before the bound is stated.

complexity.description: Time and space, e.g. 'O(n) time, O(1) space'.

tradeoff.description: When you would pick this over the submitted approach, and what it costs.

alternatives.description: At least two genuinely different approaches, not minor variations of the submitted one.

explanation.description: Why this problem belongs to the category, grounded in its structure. Explain before naming.

category.description: The underlying pattern, e.g. 'sliding window', 'monotonic stack', 'two pointers'.

problem_type.description: The kind of problem and what makes it similar — always provided.

named_example.description: A specific problem name ONLY if confident it exists and matches; otherwise null.

similar_problems.description: At least three. Describe the type; name a specific problem only when confident.

interviewer_followup.description: One sharp follow-up an interviewer would ask after this solution — one that probes a weakness or pushes toward optimization.
